title: landolphia pyramidata
intro: Landolphia pyramidata, also known as the African climbing yew or wild yew, is a species of evergreen climbing plant native to tropical Africa. It has been found to have various medicinal properties, including antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Landolphia pyramidata include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are traditionally used in folk medicine in Africa.
